## Summary

Draft implementation plan for Revise patch lifecycle E2E proof.

This PR must be implemented only after PR #850 is merged.

## Goal

Prove the full safe Revise patch lifecycle:

target passage -> choose A/B/C/custom -> preview -> apply -> new manuscript version -> audit record

This is a proof PR only. It must not introduce new product behavior.

## Dependencies

Requires PR #849 merged:

- Revise Card Contract wired into queue/cockpit.
- ready_for_revise vs needs_targeting routing.
- operation-aware labels.
- copy-ready candidate text controls.

Requires PR #850 merged:

- preview-before-apply.
- non-destructive patch application.
- manuscript version creation.
- source hash / anchor conflict detection.
- audit records.
- custom patch support.

## Scope

- Add E2E or integration proof for the Revise patch lifecycle.
- Verify A/B/C apply path.
- Verify custom apply path.
- Verify preview is required before apply.
- Verify original manuscript version remains unchanged.
- Verify new manuscript version is created.
- Verify audit record is written.
- Verify source hash conflict blocks apply.
- Verify Needs Targeting items cannot be applied.
- Verify high-risk operations remain blocked or preview-only.

## Out of scope

- No new prompt behavior.
- No evaluation pipeline behavior.
- No TrustedPath changes.
- No queue generation changes except test fixtures.
- No cockpit redesign.
- No bulk apply.

## Deterministic fixture passage

Use:

The lawyer read the clause again, and found it no less monstrous at the second reading.

Primary operation:

insert_after_selected_passage

## Required proof assertions

- Ready Revise card appears in live queue.
- Needs Targeting card cannot be applied.
- Candidate A/B/C text is actual copy-ready prose.
- Preview is mandatory before apply.
- Applying A/B/C creates a new manuscript version.
- Original manuscript version remains unchanged.
- Audit record is written.
- Custom text follows the same preview/apply path.
- Empty or meta custom text is rejected.
- Source hash mismatch blocks apply.
- Conflict status is recorded as conflict_detected.
- High-risk operations are blocked or preview-only.
